assignment.title: Group Presentation: Marine Ecosystem Deep Dive

assignment:
## Group Presentation: Marine Ecosystem Deep Dive
**Points:** 20 | **Presented:** Day 9 | **Slides due:** End of Day 9
---
### Overview
Working in teams of 3–4, you will research a marine ecosystem of your choice and present a 10–15 minute deep dive to the class on **Day 9**. Your presentation should integrate concepts from across the course.
---
### Choose One Ecosystem
- Kelp forest - Seagrass meadow - Polar sea ice ecosystem - Open-ocean oxygen minimum zone - Seamount community - Intertidal zone - Submarine canyon
---
### Presentation Requirements
Your presentation must address all of the following:
1. **Physical environment** — key abiotic factors (light, temperature, salinity, pressure, nutrients) 2. **Biodiversity** — characteristic species across at least three trophic levels 3. **Ecological processes** — at least one energy flow or nutrient cycle specific to this ecosystem 4. **Threats** — at least two human-driven threats with evidence of impact 5. **Conservation** — one current or proposed conservation strategy and your team's assessment of its feasibility
---
### Grading Criteria
| Criterion | Points | |-----------|--------| | Scientific accuracy and depth | 8 | | Integration of course concepts | 4 | | Clarity and organization of presentation | 4 | | Team participation and Q&A | 4 |
---
### Submission
Upload your slide deck (PDF or PowerPoint) to this assignment by end of Day 9.
